title: Grammatophyllum scriptum

intro: Grammatophyllum scriptum, commonly known as the "orchid of the gods," is a species of epiphytic orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Grammatophyllum scriptum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
